_provenance: Each payload entry encodes a UniProt/RefSeq sequence reference, payload length, GC properties (estimated from canonical source organism CDS), and OE-relevance pointer. Sequences themselves are NOT stored here — the analysis works on payload metadata + chassis profile.
_note: Codon Adaptation Index (CAI) is computed analytically from each payload's source-organism codon bias relative to F. prausnitzii (Sharp & Li 1987 method). Native source CDS GC% is the relevant comparator for codon-optimization burden, not protein-level GC%.

uricase.name: Urate oxidase (uricase)
uricase.source_native_codon_table: A. flavus (eukaryotic / fungal, ~50% GC genomic; CDS slightly biased toward 52%)
uricase.oe_relevance: Primary OE payload. Gut-luminal urate degradation — the rasburicase mechanism (clinical proven IV; oral ALLN-346 in Phase 2a). Anaerobic-resident chassis would deliver continuously rather than as transit organism.
uricase.load_bearing_caveat_for_lbp_chassis: Uricase requires molecular oxygen as a substrate (catalyzes uric acid + O2 + H2O -> 5-hydroxyisourate + H2O2). F. prausnitzii lumen is essentially anoxic. This is a fundamental physiological mismatch that the LBP-track framing must address — the host can express the protein but the enzyme reaction needs O2.
uricase.verification_note: UniProt Q00511 (verified via REST 2026-05-16): A. flavus urate oxidase, 302 aa, 34,241 Da, cofactorless oxidoreductase, catalyzes uric acid hydroxylation in presence of molecular O2. Mechanism is intrinsically O2-dependent — not a 'we should add O2' problem, a chemistry problem.

lactoferrin.name: Human lactoferrin
lactoferrin.source_native_codon_table: H. sapiens (mammalian, biased toward higher GC)
lactoferrin.glycosylation_note: N-glycans NOT required for iron-binding or bacteriostatic function. Poly-N-acetyllactosaminic carbohydrate moiety needed for TLR4 activation (immunomodulatory). For LBP-luminal iron sequestration delivery, aglycosylated may suffice.
lactoferrin.oe_relevance: TNFα-cycle relief payload (per engineered-lbp-chassis.md). Already characterized in comp-005 (koji protease stability) and comp-034 (linker redesign). For LBP chassis: continuous luminal release.
lactoferrin.verification_note: UniProt P02788 (verified via REST 2026-05-16): 710 aa total, signal peptide 1-19, mature 691 aa, 17 disulfide bonds, 3 N-glycosylation sites. Function: bacteriostasis via iron sequestration.

scr1_sushi_domain_truncation.name: Soluble CR1 (sCR1) — SCR1-4 truncation (per H05 / comp-012)
scr1_sushi_domain_truncation.n_glycosylation_sites_scr1_to_4: approximately 3-4 (estimated, fraction of 22 over the SCR1-4 region)
scr1_sushi_domain_truncation.oe_relevance: CP0 complement-priming chokepoint payload. comp-006/012 evaluated koji protease stability for the SCR1-4 truncation (HIGH risk in koji; LBP chassis avoids the koji protease environment entirely per engineered-lbp-chassis.md).
scr1_sushi_domain_truncation.verification_note: UniProt P17927 (verified via REST 2026-05-16): 2039 aa full length, 60 disulfides total across 30 SCR domains (2 per domain — canonical sushi/CCP fold per the DAF SCR1-4 lesson). 22 N-glycosylation sites. SCR1-4 truncation length 280 aa, 8 disulfides — load-bearing per the comp-012 lesson.

butyryl_coa_acetate_coa_transferase.name: Butyryl-CoA:acetate CoA-transferase (native pathway boost)
butyryl_coa_acetate_coa_transferase.source_organism: Faecalibacterium duncaniae (= F. prausnitzii A2-165 in older nomenclature)
butyryl_coa_acetate_coa_transferase.oe_relevance: Butyrate-pathway boost — overexpressing the rate-limiting acetate-to-butyryl-CoA committing enzyme drives flux through the butyrate biosynthesis pathway. Butyrate is the dual-action ABCG2 inducer (PPARgamma + Q141K HDACi rescue) per engineered-lbp-chassis.md.
butyryl_coa_acetate_coa_transferase.verification_note: UniProt C7H5K4 (verified via REST 2026-05-16): F. duncaniae DSM 17677 / JCM 31915 / A2-165, gene FAEPRAA2165_01575, 448 aa, EC 2.8.3.-, catalyzes butanoate + acetyl-CoA -> butanoyl-CoA + acetate. Cytoplasmic (no signal peptide). NATIVE PAYLOAD — codon usage is by definition perfect (CAI = 1.0).
